title: China is winning the other tech race
gist: In this free teaser, Smith frames great-power status as mastery of the era's key technologies, argues the US still leads AI (an 8-10 month edge that is widening, bigger than the headline 4 months), but flags that China is clearly dominating the parallel electric-technology revolution.

insights.claim: Per Paul Kennedy's framework, nations become great powers by mastering the most important technologies of their day (gunpowder, steam, mass production, electricity, airplanes), and to that 20th-century list one should add semiconductors, computers, and the internet.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: Gives a lens for ranking geopolitical winners by which country masters the defining technologies (today: AI, electric tech, biotech).
insights.quote: countries become great powers due to their mastery of the most important technologies of the day
insights.locator: The most interesting idea is that

insights.claim: Leading nations can fall behind not only through hubris and overstretch but through technological disruption, as when Britain's industrial revolution made Dutch mercantile trade less pivotal and Britain itself later missed aviation and combustion-engine dominance.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: Reframes great-power decline as missing a technology wave, the risk Smith implies the US faces in electric tech.
insights.quote: leading nations fall behind due to technological disruption
insights.locator: Although he attributes great-power decline to hubris

insights.claim: America's AI lead over China is bigger than the headline four months: on private (non-public) benchmarks the lead nearly doubles to roughly eight-to-ten months, because Chinese open-source models tend to overscore on public benchmarks.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: The true US-China model gap is larger than market narratives suggest, supporting US frontier-lab and semiconductor dominance, but it is still only ~10 months.
insights.quote: on private tests, America's lead nearly doubled, to eight to ten months

insights.claim: NIST data shows Chinese AI models have been falling further behind US models lately, reversing the catching-up narrative.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: Divergence (not convergence) in model capability is bullish for the US AI stack and validates the export-control strategy.
insights.quote: Chinese models have been falling further behind lately
insights.locator: And here's a chart from NIST

insights.claim: The US AI lead rests on pioneering research plus mastery of semiconductors (with its allies) and skillful, timely use of export controls.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: Names the three pillars (research, semis+allies, export controls) whose erosion would be the leading indicator of a lost US AI lead.
insights.quote: its mastery of semiconductors (along with its network of allies) and its skillful and timely use of export controls
insights.locator: Right now, the United States is leading

insights.claim: Even a ten-month AI lead is slender, and slightly out-of-date frontier models will still have awesome capabilities and can make decent money as cheaper alternatives.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: Implies a durable commercial market for fast-follower/cheaper models even without frontier parity, relevant to open-source and Chinese model monetization.
insights.quote: even slightly out-of-date models will have truly awesome capabilities — and will probably be able to make decent amounts of money as cheaper alternatives
insights.locator: Ten months is a slender lead

insights.claim: Beyond AI there are two other major technology revolutions underway, electric technology and biotech, and China is clearly dominating the electric-technology one.
insights.speaker: Noah Smith
insights.so_what: Flags electric tech (EVs, batteries, grid) as a domain where China, not the US, holds the great-power-defining lead, a strategic vulnerability for the US.
insights.quote: the one that China is clearly dominating: electric technology
insights.locator: focus on the one that China is clearly dominating
